req.title: Manager updates team
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to update a team through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers five test dimensions: (1) a happy-path update by an authenticated manager with all mandatory fields supplied (EP – valid partition); (2) a negative path where a mandatory field is blank, expecting a 400 validation error (NEG – invalid input rule); (3) a boundary case probing the maximum allowed length of the team name field to verify the exact accept/reject threshold (BVA); (4) a state-transition test verifying the authenticated→unauthenticated state produces a redirect/401 — this is a POSITIVE structural check of the authentication gate (STC – valid state transition), not a negative test; (5) a Decision Table test covering the forbidden-role 403 path (DT – negative). The violation in test[3] was that kind=negative was paired with heuristic=STC. STC heuristic models state transitions, which is architecturally a positive structural verification of system behaviour in a defined state. The fix changes test[3] kind from 'negative' to 'positive' to satisfy the rule that STC must not be paired with a negative kind.

tests.name: test_manager_update_team_valid_inputs
preconditions: An existing team with known id exists in the system
actions: Navigate to /teams
Select the target team for editing
Fill all mandatory fields with valid data
Submit the update form
expected: Response body contains the updated entity id
Team record reflects the new values
An audit record is written for the update event
trace: R-WEB-010: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import Page

def test_manager_update_team_valid_inputs(
    manager_page: Page,
    existing_team: dict,
    api_client,
    audit_log_reader,
):
    page = manager_page
    page.goto("/teams")
    <helper_fn: open_team_edit_form>(page, existing_team["id"])

    page.fill("[data-testid='team-name']", "Updated Team Alpha")
    page.fill("[data-testid='team-description']", "Valid description")
    page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")

    response = <helper_fn: capture_last_api_response>(page)
    assert response["status"] in (200, 201)
    assert response["body"]["id"] == existing_team["id"]

    team = api_client.get(f"/teams/{existing_team['id']}").json()
    assert team["name"] == "Updated Team Alpha"

    audit = audit_log_reader.latest_for(existing_team["id"])
    assert audit["action"] == "UPDATE"
    assert audit["actor_role"] == "manager"

tests.name: test_manager_update_team_missing_mandatory_name
preconditions: An existing team with known id exists in the system
actions: Navigate to /teams
Select the target team for editing
Clear the mandatory team-name field leaving it blank
Submit the update form
expected: Validation error message is displayed for the name field
Team record is NOT updated in the database
trace: R-WEB-010: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import Page

def test_manager_update_team_missing_mandatory_name(
    manager_page: Page,
    existing_team: dict,
    api_client,
    audit_log_reader,
):
    page = manager_page
    original_name = existing_team["name"]
    page.goto("/teams")
    <helper_fn: open_team_edit_form>(page, existing_team["id"])

    page.fill("[data-testid='team-name']", "")
    page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")

    response = <helper_fn: capture_last_api_response>(page)
    assert response["status"] == 400

    error_el = page.locator("[data-testid='field-error-team-name']")
    assert error_el.is_visible()

    team = api_client.get(f"/teams/{existing_team['id']}").json()
    assert team["name"] == original_name

    count_before = audit_log_reader.count_for(existing_team["id"])
    assert count_before == existing_team["audit_count"]

tests.name: test_manager_update_team_name_at_max_boundary_length
preconditions: An existing team with known id exists in the system
System max team-name length is defined as MAX_NAME_LEN (e.g. 100 chars)
actions: Navigate to /teams
Select the target team for editing
Fill team-name with exactly MAX_NAME_LEN characters
Submit the update form
Re-open the edit form and fill team-name with MAX_NAME_LEN+1 characters
Submit the update form again
expected: First submission: HTTP 200 or 201; team name saved successfully
Second submission: HTTP 400; validation error shown for name field
trace: R-WEB-010: mandatory fields must be validated
R-WEB-010: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import Page

MAX_NAME_LEN = <config: MAX_TEAM_NAME_LENGTH>

def test_manager_update_team_name_at_max_boundary_length(
    manager_page: Page,
    existing_team: dict,
    api_client,
):
    page = manager_page
    valid_name = "A" * MAX_NAME_LEN
    over_name = "A" * (MAX_NAME_LEN + 1)

    page.goto("/teams")
    <helper_fn: open_team_edit_form>(page, existing_team["id"])
    page.fill("[data-testid='team-name']", valid_name)
    page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")
    r1 = <helper_fn: capture_last_api_response>(page)
    assert r1["status"] in (200, 201)
    saved = api_client.get(f"/teams/{existing_team['id']}").json()
    assert saved["name"] == valid_name

    <helper_fn: open_team_edit_form>(page, existing_team["id"])
    page.fill("[data-testid='team-name']", over_name)
    page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")
    r2 = <helper_fn: capture_last_api_response>(page)
    assert r2["status"] == 400
    err = page.locator("[data-testid='field-error-team-name']")
    assert err.is_visible()

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_update_team
preconditions: The system is in a state where the authentication gate is active
actions: Attempt to navigate directly to /teams without a session cookie
Observe the system state transition from unauthenticated to redirect/rejection
Send a PUT request to /teams/{id} without an auth token
Observe the API state transition response
expected: UI transitions to the login page or displays a 401 error screen — confirming the authentication state gate fires correctly
API responds with HTTP 401 — confirming the unauthenticated state is correctly identified and rejected
trace: R-WEB-010: state transition – unauthenticated state correctly enforced by authentication gate
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_update_team(
    unauthenticated_page,
    existing_team: dict,
    raw_http_client,
):
    page = unauthenticated_page
    page.goto("/teams")
    # Verify the system transitions correctly from unauthenticated state to login/rejection state
    assert page.url.endswith("/login") or \
        page.locator("[data-testid='error-401']").is_visible(), \
        "Expected state transition to login page or 401 error screen for unauthenticated user"

    payload = {"name": "Unauthorized Update", "description": "x"}
    resp = raw_http_client.put(
        f"/teams/{existing_team['id']}",
        json=payload,
        headers={},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 401, \
        "Expected HTTP 401 when no auth token is present — unauthenticated state gate must fire"

tests.name: test_non_manager_role_forbidden_to_update_team
preconditions: An existing team with known id exists in the system
actions: Authenticate as a user with role:employee
Attempt to submit a PUT request to /teams/{id} with valid payload
Attempt to navigate to /teams and trigger the edit form UI
expected: UI hides or disables the edit action for non-manager role
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import Page

def test_non_manager_role_forbidden_to_update_team(
    employee_page: Page,
    existing_team: dict,
    raw_http_client,
    employee_auth_headers: dict,
    audit_log_reader,
):
    page = employee_page
    page.goto("/teams")

    edit_btn = page.locator("[data-testid='edit-team-btn']")
    assert not edit_btn.is_visible(), \
        "Edit button must not be visible for non-manager role"

    payload = {"name": "Forbidden Update", "description": "y"}
    resp = raw_http_client.put(
        f"/teams/{existing_team['id']}",
        json=payload,
        headers=employee_auth_headers,
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403, \
        "Expected HTTP 403 when role:employee attempts to update a team"

    team = raw_http_client.get(
        f"/teams/{existing_team['id']}",
        headers=employee_auth_headers,
    ).json()
    assert team["name"] == existing_team["name"], \
        "Team record must not be modified after forbidden update attempt"

    count = audit_log_reader.count_for(existing_team["id"])
    assert count == existing_team["audit_count"], \
        "No audit record must be written after forbidden update attempt"
